rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO CONTRA COSTA COUNTY** GUARDIANSHIP OF HAYDEN ANGELO DE BARROS CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) On Schedule F - Liabilities there are 2 entries for Costco 2018 food for $8,526.00 and 2019 $6,810.00. The court requested receipts for these entries. Guardian attached Costco receipts for 2/1/2018 to 12/31/2020 to the Third Supplement. It appears during 2018 and 2019 $4,734.20 in purchases were made for gas, many $2.00 unknown entries, adult gummies, shipping tape, centrum silver men/women 50+, melatonin, wine, and vodka. It appears starting 01/03/2020 which is not covered by the entries on Schedule F - Liabilities $1,237.31 in purchases were made for tires, 50 inch tv, gas, centrum silver men 50+, and other unknown $2.00 entries. 2) On 04/08/2019 the court order page 2 line 6 states, "No other reimbursement for childcare are allowed without prior court approval. No court approval was sought for $5,385.00 in childcare expenses already reimbursed in this account. Examiner requested receipts for all entries. 3) On 04/08/2019 the court order page 2 line 9 states "Refund of $3,753.74 for life insurance premium paid shall be reflected in the next account." No such refund appears in the account. In the Supplement filed 07/21/202 page 1 lines 19-20 allege the guardian made a deposit of $3,753.53 to the Guardianship estate account which appears to be the wrong amount. 4) Examiner requested the legal basis for the guardianship paying legal expenses related to setting up the ward's inheritance estate. No legal authority provided. Examiner requested attorney timesheets for all legal expenses paid to ARCR. No timesheets provided. 5) Examiner requested legal authority for including the items listed on Schedule F Liabilities and to file receipts for all entries. No legal authority provided. No receipts provided for many entries on Schedule F Liabilities.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VENRICE PALMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/19/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The First Supplement filed 02/07/2022 now alleges the assets on hand on March 31, 2021 is a TD Ameritrade Account #4329 with a balance of $344.52. Even though there has been a waiver of account the Examiner must be able to trace the funds from the I&A value of $1,443,843.54 to the assets on hand of $344.52. Please explain what happened to $1,443,499.02 in estate assets. 2) This issue still hasn't been addressed. On the attorney timesheets please explain why there are the same entries on both the ordinary and extraordinary timesheets. See entries on the extraordinary timesheets for 6/14/2016, 6/15/2016, 06/07/2016, 08/05/2016, and 2 entries on 11/23/2016. It appears the extraordinary request should be reduced by these duplicate entries as they do not appear extraordinary. 3) This issue still hasn't been addressed. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.35 I. 6) a.] 4) This issue still hasn't been addressed. The proposed order must state specifically what assets are being distributed only alleging distribute the assets of the estate as follows is not sufficient. 5) This issue still hasn't been addressed. There are no exhibits attached to the filed petition. Please file either a Supplement or Amendment with notice that provides the Exhibits that are described in the petition but not attached. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/4/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LILIA M. MARTINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Attoney Fees And Costs And For A Lien Against The Conservatees Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petition was filed approximately two months after the Court issued an order terminating the conservatorship. Notice of the petition to terminate the conservatorship upon approval of a final account of the conservator of the estate was given to the petitioner. The petitioner objected to the petition and appeared at the hearing on the petition to terminate without addressing his reimbursement request. 2) The petitioner's request for reimbursement includes fees for his attorney's legal services rendered after Maria Grayum was appointed as conservator of the estate in December 2017. Neither the petitioner's attorney nor the conservator of the estate joined in the petition. See Probate Code sections 2640(a)(3); 2640.1(a)(b)(2); 2641 & 2642. 3) The petition requests the imposition of a lien on the conservatee's real property and other personal property already distributed to the personal representative of the conservatee's estate, to secure the fees and costs sought by the petition with 5% simple annual interest accruing from the date of the Court's order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTON BAJJALIEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) 03/02/2022 Report Regarding Settlement alleges settlement discussions have started but petitioner does not agree to a continuance. Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Issues in Michelle Bajjalieh Petition of Lost Will: [Same issues since 4/21/2021.] a. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] In response attorney E. Neal McGettigan filed a Declaration on 05/10/2021 that is not signed or verified by Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh. [See CRC 7.103] b. Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh has not submitted a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [See LRSF 14.7]. 3) Issues in First Amended Petition for Letters of Administration [Filed 08/11/2021 by Jacqueline Ahwal] a. DE-121 Notice was not given 15 days prior to the hearing to the following individuals directly but was given 1 day prior to the 10/6/2021 hearing: Michelle Bajjalieh, Matthew Joseph Bajjalieh, Terah Bajjalieh, and Debra Dolch.

ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) 03/02/2022 Report Regarding Settlement alleges settlement discussions have started but petitioner does not agree to a continuance. Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Issues in Michelle Bajjalieh Petition of Lost Will: [Same issues since 4/21/2021.] a. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] In response attorney E. Neal McGettigan filed a Declaration on 05/10/2021 that is not signed or verified by Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh. [See CRC 7.103] b. Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh has not submitted a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [See LRSF 14.7]. 3) Issues in First Amended Petition for Letters of Administration [Filed 08/11/2021 by Jacqueline Ahwal] a. DE-121 Notice was not given 15 days prior to the hearing to the following individuals directly but was given 1 day prior to the 10/6/2021 hearing: Michelle Bajjalieh, Matthew Joseph Bajjalieh, Terah Bajjalieh, and Debra Dolch.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARY EVERETT MOATS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Paragraph 3 of the published notice stated that: "The petition for probate requests that EXECUTOR be appointed as personal representative to administer the estate of the decedent." See Probate Code section 8100. 2) Item 4 of the mailed notice (DE-121 form) did not specify the "lost" status of the will sought to be admitted to probate. See LRSF 14.20. 3) The petitioner has a complete copy of the will received from the decedent, but the original will cannot be located. 4) The petitioner happens to be an attorney who intends to represent herself as executor in this proceeding. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHIRLEY S.M. CHONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/19/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner must file Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 2) William Chong must comply with summons in Probate Code 8250. 3) William Chong please file the alleged 03/08/2021 original will in compliance with Probate Code 8200. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/4/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
